hints.'Qui' and 'fit': 'Qui' is an interrogative adverb meaning 'how' — only 'fit' is a verb from this pair
hints.'nemo' and 'contentus': 'nemo' is a pronoun meaning 'no one' and 'contentus' is an adjective meaning 'content'
hints.'ut' and 'sit': 'ut' is a conjunction meaning 'that' — only 'sit' is a verb from this pair

hints.to do: 'to do' is 'facere' — 'fit' comes from 'fieri' (to become/be made)
hints.to happen: While 'fit' can mean 'it happens', the infinitive 'fieri' primarily means 'to become/be made'

hints.Imperfect: Imperfect of 'fieri' 3rd person would be 'fiebat' — 'fit' is the present form
hints.Perfect: Perfect of 'fieri' 3rd person would be 'factus est' — 'fit' is present
hints.Future: Future of 'fieri' 3rd person would be 'fiet' — 'fit' is the present form

hints.fido, fidere, fisus sum, —: These are the principal parts of 'fidere' (to trust)
hints.fingo, fingere, finxi, fictum: These are the principal parts of 'fingere' (to shape/invent)
hints.fugio, fugere, fugi, fugitum: These are the principal parts of 'fugere' (to flee)

hints.to sit: 'to sit' is 'sedere' — 'sit' is the subjunctive of 'esse' (to be)
hints.to allow: 'to allow' is 'sinere' — 'sit' is the present subjunctive of 'esse'

hints.Indicative: Indicative present 3rd person of 'esse' would be 'est' — 'sit' has the subjunctive vowel
hints.Imperative: Imperative of 'esse' would be 'es' (sg.) or 'este' (pl.) — 'sit' is subjunctive
hints.Infinitive: Infinitive of 'esse' is 'esse' — 'sit' is a conjugated subjunctive form

hints.sedeo, sedere, sedi, sessum: These are the principal parts of 'sedere' (to sit)
hints.sino, sinere, sivi, situm: These are the principal parts of 'sinere' (to allow)
hints.sto, stare, steti, statum: These are the principal parts of 'stare' (to stand)

hints.anyone: 'anyone' is 'quisquam' — 'nemo' is the definite negative 'no one'

hints.Genitive: Genitive of 'contentus' would be 'contenti' — 'contentus' is the nominative form
hints.Accusative: Accusative of 'contentus' would be 'contentum' — 'contentus' is nominative, predicate adjective
hints.Ablative: Ablative of 'contentus' would be 'contento' — 'contentus' is the nominative form

hints.Relative pronoun meaning 'who': 'qui' as relative pronoun introduces a relative clause — here it begins a question meaning 'how'
hints.Indefinite pronoun meaning 'anyone': 'qui' as indefinite means 'anyone' after 'si/ne/num' — here it is interrogative
hints.Conjunction meaning 'because': 'qui' is not used as a conjunction — here it is an interrogative adverb

translation: How is it that no one is content
